PROOF OF SERVICE OF REPLY IN SUPPORT OF RESPONDENT'S DEMURRER TO CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR, INC.'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF RESPONDENT'S REPLY FILED BY RESPONDENT BOARD OF APPEALS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERVED DEC-09-2008, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO REAL PARTY IN INTEREST LEE, CHEOL HOON LEE, BUJA LEE, TONY

LAW AND MOTION 302, REAL PARTIES IN INTEREST CHEOL HOON LEE, BUJA LEE AND TONY LEE'S JOINDER TO DEMURRER FILED BY CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO TO CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR, INC.'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. RULING: DISCUSSED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED FOR PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE A BENEFICIAL INTEREST. PLAINTIFF'S INTEREST CONSISTED OF HAVING A PERMIT TO REMOVE THE SIGNS; ONCE REMOVED PLAINTIFF HAD NO FURTHER INTEREST. COMPETITION WITH THE LEE'S IS NOT A SUFFICIENT INTEREST. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT DEMONSTRATE STANDING. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring To Trial
Off Calendar Sep-19-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring To Trial
Off Calendar Sep-12-2024 Continued To Oct-17-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT BOARD OF APPEALS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring To Trial.

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jan-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT BOARD OF APPEALS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR, INC.'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.
APPEARANCES: Kenneth B. Finney, Esq. of Beveridge & Diamond, P.C. 262-4000 for petitioner & opposing party (did not comply with the tentative ruling rule re giving proper notice to opposing party; gave late notice); no other appearances.
RULING: Discussed and the Court modified its tentative ruling and adopted as follows: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED FOR PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE A BENEFICIAL INTEREST. PLAINTIFF'S INTEREST CONSISTED OF HAVING A PERMIT TO REMOVE THE SIGNS; ONCE REMOVED PLAINTIFF HAD NO FURTHER INTEREST. COMPETITION WITH THE LEE'S IS NOT A SUFFICIENT INTEREST. PLAINTIFF's REQUEST for LEAVE TO AMEND is granted to allege facts that demonstrate standing. (302/PJM/BD) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
